feature.label: Eclipse GenAI LOC Tracker
feature.description:
Eclipse GenAI LOC Tracker
Tracks Lines of Code (LOC) generated with GitHub Copilot and other GenAI assistants inside the Eclipse IDE. Events captured by this plugin are posted to the Spring Boot LOC service that is also used by the companion IntelliJ plugin, providing a unified view of AI-assisted productivity across both IDEs.
Features: - Captures inline accept / tab-completion events from Copilot - Aggregates LOC counts per file, project, and session - Posts metrics asynchronously to the configured LOC service endpoint - Honors workspace-level enable/disable preferences
feature.copyright: Copyright (c) 2026 Cognizant Technology Solutions (CTS). All rights reserved.
feature.license:
Eclipse GenAI LOC Tracker Copyright (c) 2026 Cognizant Technology Solutions. All rights reserved.
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"); you may not use this software except in compliance with the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at
https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
USAGE ----- This plugin instruments the Eclipse IDE to track AI-assisted lines of code (LOC) generated by GitHub Copilot and other GenAI tooling. Telemetry is collected per Cognizant's internal data-handling policies and is intended for productivity analytics only. No source code content is transmitted off the developer's machine; only aggregate LOC counts and event metadata are sent to the configured Spring Boot LOC service.
DATA COLLECTED -------------- - Number of AI-suggested lines accepted / rejected - File extension and project name (no file contents) - Timestamp and IDE session identifier - User identifier (corporate email / SSO id) for attribution
DISCLAIMER ---------- Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the License for the specific language governing permissions and limitations under the License.
By clicking "I accept the terms of the license agreement" you acknowledge that usage metrics will be collected by this plugin in accordance with Cognizant's internal data-handling policies.
